title: Login Token
description: The canonical shape of a LoginTokens record — docs/29-PHASE-2A-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §4's already-locked sheet, and the single-use artifact that resolves a patient's registered email to a patient_id and, once consumed, a Session (docs/33 §1.3, ADR-002, ADR-003). Only the token's hash is ever part of this shape — the raw token itself is never stored, never part of this schema, and exists only transiently in memory between generation and delivery. See login-token.md for the hashing rationale and the used_at sentinel-value note.

token_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (64 hex characters) of the raw, single-use login token. The raw token is never stored anywhere, including here — this field exists specifically so a stolen datastore export can never be used to authenticate (ADR-010).

patient_id.description: The Patient Identity this token, once consumed, resolves to (docs/33 §1.2, ADR-002). Set once at issuance, never modified.

issued_at.description: The instant this token was generated. Set once, never modified.

expires_at.description: The instant this token becomes permanently invalid, regardless of use. No renewal — a caller presenting an expired token must request a new one (docs/29 §3: "checks not-expired").

used_at.description: Empty string until this single-use token is consumed exactly once; a non-empty value is the ISO 8601 instant it was consumed (docs/29 §3: "not-already-used, single-use, enforced server-side"). Deliberately NOT format:"date-time"-constrained at the schema level — an empty string is the documented, intentional "not yet used" sentinel, consistent with FoundationDataStore.gs's existing empty-cell convention for every other Foundation entity. See login-token.md.
